name: operating_model_prose_system
outcome_goal: Build the prose-based operating model system: reference catalog, compiler, API endpoints, document editor, and dashboard integration

jobs.label: reference_catalog_migration
jobs.prompt:
## Objective
Create the reference catalog migration and auto-populate it from existing data.

## Working Directory
`Code&DBs/Databases/migrations/workflow/`

## Create: `034_reference_catalog.sql`

```sql
-- Reference catalog: addressable slugs for the operating model prose language
-- @source/action for integrations, #type/field for objects, agent-name for agents

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS reference_catalog (
    slug TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    ref_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    display_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    resolved_id TEXT,
    resolved_table TEXT,
    schema JSONB DEFAULT '{}',
    description TEXT DEFAULT '',
    created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_ref_catalog_type ON reference_catalog (ref_type);

-- Auto-populate from integration_registry
INSERT INTO reference_catalog (slug, ref_type, display_name, resolved_id, resolved_table, description)
SELECT
    '@' || ir.id || '/' || cap->>'action' AS slug,
    'integration' AS ref_type,
    ir.name || ': ' || cap->>'action' AS display_name,
    ir.id AS resolved_id,
    'integration_registry' AS resolved_table,
    cap->>'description' AS description
FROM integration_registry ir,
     jsonb_array_elements(CASE WHEN jsonb_typeof(ir.capabilities) = 'array' THEN ir.capabilities ELSE '[]'::jsonb END) AS cap
WHERE cap->>'action' IS NOT NULL
ON CONFLICT (slug) DO NOTHING;

-- Also add integration-level references (without action)
INSERT INTO reference_catalog (slug, ref_type, display_name, resolved_id, resolved_table, description)
SELECT
    '@' || id AS slug,
    'integration' AS ref_type,
    name AS display_name,
    id AS resolved_id,
    'integration_registry' AS resolved_table,
    description
FROM integration_registry
ON CONFLICT (slug) DO NOTHING;

-- Auto-populate from object_types
INSERT INTO reference_catalog (slug, ref_type, display_name, resolved_id, resolved_table, description)
SELECT
    '#' || type_id AS slug,
    'object' AS ref_type,
    name AS display_name,
    type_id AS resolved_id,
    'object_types' AS resolved_table,
    description
FROM object_types
ON CONFLICT (slug) DO NOTHING;

-- Auto-populate object fields from property_definitions
INSERT INTO reference_catalog (slug, ref_type, display_name, resolved_id, resolved_table, schema, description)
SELECT
    '#' || ot.type_id || '/' || prop->>'name' AS slug,
    'object' AS ref_type,
    ot.name || '.' || prop->>'name' AS display_name,
    ot.type_id AS resolved_id,
    'object_types' AS resolved_table,
    jsonb_build_object('field_type', prop->>'type', 'required', COALESCE(prop->>'required', 'false')) AS schema,
    COALESCE(prop->>'description', '') AS description
FROM object_types ot,
     jsonb_array_elements(CASE WHEN jsonb_typeof(ot.property_definitions) = 'array' THEN ot.property_definitions ELSE '[]'::jsonb END) AS prop
WHERE prop->>'name' IS NOT NULL
ON CONFLICT (slug) DO NOTHING;

-- Auto-populate agent routes from task_type_routing
INSERT INTO reference_catalog (slug, ref_type, display_name, resolved_id, resolved_table, description)
SELECT DISTINCT
    task_type AS slug,
    'agent' AS ref_type,
    task_type || ' (' || provider_slug || '/' || model_slug || ')' AS display_name,
    provider_slug || '/' || model_slug AS resolved_id,
    'task_type_routing' AS resolved_table,
    'Agent route: ' || task_type
FROM task_type_routing
WHERE tier = 1
ON CONFLICT (slug) DO NOTHING;
```

This creates the reference_catalog table and populates it from existing integration_registry, object_types, and task_type_routing data.

## IMPORTANT
- Use ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING to be idempotent
- Handle the case where capabilities might not be a JSON array (use CASE WHEN jsonb_typeof)
- Handle the case where property_definitions might not be a JSON array

jobs.label: compiler_backend
jobs.prompt:
## Objective
Build the compilation pipeline: prose → LLM compilation → reference extraction → resolution → execution plan.

## Working Directory
`Code&DBs/Workflow/runtime/`

## Context
The reference_catalog table has: slug, ref_type (integration|object|agent), display_name, resolved_id, resolved_table, schema, description.

The integration_registry table has: id, name, provider, capabilities (JSONB array of {action, returns, description}), auth_status.
The object_types table has: type_id, name, description, property_definitions (JSONB array).
The task_type_routing table has: task_type (e.g., 'auto/build'), provider_slug, model_slug, tier.

The existing `call_llm` function in `adapters/llm_client.py` sends requests to LLM APIs (Anthropic/OpenAI). It takes an `LLMRequest` with messages, system_prompt, tools, etc.

The existing `pipelineToQueueSpec` in the frontend compiles PipelineModel → QueueSpec. We need a Python equivalent for the backend.

## Create: `runtime/compiler.py`

The compilation pipeline:

```python
"""Operating model compiler.

Converts natural language prose into structured operating models with
resolvable references (@integrations, #objects, {variables}, agent-names).
"""

import json
import re
import logging
import uuid
from typing import Any

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


def compile_prose(prose: str, pg: Any) -> dict:
    """Compile user prose into a structured operating model.
    
    Steps:
    1. Load available references from reference_catalog
    2. Load integration details
    3. Call LLM to compile prose into structured version with @/#/{} references
    4. Extract and resolve references
    5. Generate execution plan (QueueSpec)
    
    Returns {
        compiled_prose: str,
        references: list[dict],
        execution_plan: dict (QueueSpec format),
        unresolved: list[str],
        title: str,
    }
    """
    # 1. Load available references
    catalog = _load_catalog(pg)
    integrations = _load_integrations(pg)
    object_types = _load_object_types(pg)
    
    # 2. Call LLM to compile
    compiled = _llm_compile(prose, catalog, integrations, object_types)
    
    # 3. Extract references from compiled prose
    references = _extract_references(compiled['prose'])
    
    # 4. Resolve references against catalog
    resolved, unresolved = _resolve_references(references, catalog, pg)
    
    # 5. Generate execution plan
    execution_plan = _generate_execution_plan(compiled, resolved, pg)
    
    return {
        'compiled_prose': compiled['prose'],
        'title': compiled.get('title', prose[:60]),
        'references': resolved,
        'execution_plan': execution_plan,
        'unresolved': unresolved,
        'authority': compiled.get('authority', ''),
        'sla': compiled.get('sla', {}),
    }


def _load_catalog(pg: Any) -> list[dict]:
    rows = pg.execute('SELECT slug, ref_type, display_name, resolved_id, description FROM reference_catalog ORDER BY slug')
    return [dict(r) for r in (rows or [])]


def _load_integrations(pg: Any) -> list[dict]:
    rows = pg.execute("SELECT id, name, provider, capabilities, auth_status FROM integration_registry WHERE auth_status = 'connected'")
    result = []
    for r in (rows or []):
        d = dict(r)
        caps = d.get('capabilities', '[]')
        if isinstance(caps, str):
            try: caps = json.loads(caps)
            except: caps = []
        d['capabilities'] = caps
        result.append(d)
    return result


def _load_object_types(pg: Any) -> list[dict]:
    rows = pg.execute('SELECT type_id, name, description, property_definitions FROM object_types')
    result = []
    for r in (rows or []):
        d = dict(r)
        props = d.get('property_definitions', '[]')
        if isinstance(props, str):
            try: props = json.loads(props)
            except: props = []
        d['property_definitions'] = props
        result.append(d)
    return result


def _llm_compile(prose: str, catalog: list, integrations: list, object_types: list) -> dict:
    """Call LLM to compile prose into structured version."""
    from adapters.llm_client import call_llm, LLMRequest
    import os
    
    # Build context about available references
    integration_list = '\n'.join(
        f'  @{i["id"]}: {i["name"]} ({i["provider"]}) — actions: {", ".join(c.get("action","") for c in i.get("capabilities",[]))}'
        for i in integrations
    )
    object_list = '\n'.join(
        f'  #{o["type_id"]}: {o["name"]} — fields: {", ".join(p.get("name","") for p in o.get("property_definitions",[]))}'
        for o in object_types
    )
    
    system_prompt = f"""You are an operating model compiler. You convert natural language descriptions of business processes into structured prose with executable references.

Available integrations (use @id/action syntax):
{integration_list or '  (none configured)'}

Available data objects (use #type/field syntax):
{object_list or '  (none configured)'}

Available agent routes: auto/build, auto/review, auto/architecture, auto/wiring, auto/test, auto/refactor

Rules:
- Replace vague references with specific @integration/action or #object/field references where possible
- Name agents with descriptive hyphenated names (e.g., triage-agent, quality-reviewer)
- Mark dynamic values with {{curly braces}} and include constraints (e.g., {{priority: P1|P2|P3}})
- Include Authority and SLA sections if the prose implies them
- Keep the prose readable and natural — don't make it look like code
- If an integration or object doesn't exist, still use the @ or # syntax but the system will flag it as unresolved

Respond with JSON:
{{
  "title": "short title for this operating model",
  "prose": "the compiled structured prose",
  "authority": "who has decision authority (if mentioned)",
  "sla": {{"label": "time constraint"}} (if mentioned)
}}"""
    
    # Determine which LLM to use
    api_key = os.environ.get('ANTHROPIC_API_KEY', '')
    if api_key:
        endpoint = 'https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages'
        provider = 'anthropic'
        model = 'claude-sonnet-4-5-20250514'
    else:
        api_key = os.environ.get('OPENAI_API_KEY', '')
        endpoint = 'https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions'
        provider = 'openai'
        model = 'gpt-4o'
    
    if not api_key:
        # Fallback: basic compilation without LLM
        return _basic_compile(prose)
    
    request = LLMRequest(
        endpoint_uri=endpoint,
        api_key=api_key,
        provider_slug=provider,
        model_slug=model,
        messages=(
            {'role': 'user', 'content': f'Compile this operating model description into structured prose:\n\n{prose}'},
        ),
        system_prompt=system_prompt,
        max_tokens=2000,
        temperature=0.3,
    )
    
    try:
        response = call_llm(request)
        text = response.text or ''
        # Parse JSON from response
        # Try to find JSON block
        json_match = re.search(r'\{[\s\S]*\}', text)
        if json_match:
            return json.loads(json_match.group())
        return {'title': prose[:60], 'prose': text, 'authority': '', 'sla': {}}
    except Exception as exc:
        logger.warning('LLM compilation failed: %s — falling back to basic', exc)
        return _basic_compile(prose)


def _basic_compile(prose: str) -> dict:
    """Fallback compilation without LLM — just cleans up the prose."""
    return {
        'title': prose[:60].strip(),
        'prose': prose,
        'authority': '',
        'sla': {},
    }


def _extract_references(prose: str) -> list[dict]:
    """Extract @integrations, #objects, {variables}, and agent-names from prose."""
    refs = []
    
    # @source/action references
    for m in re.finditer(r'@([\w-]+(?:/[\w-]+)*)', prose):
        refs.append({
            'id': f'ref_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}',
            'type': 'integration',
            'slug': '@' + m.group(1),
            'span': [m.start(), m.end()],
            'raw': m.group(0),
        })
    
    # #type/field references
    for m in re.finditer(r'#([\w-]+(?:/[\w-]+)*(?:/\{[^}]+\})?)', prose):
        refs.append({
            'id': f'ref_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}',
            'type': 'object',
            'slug': '#' + m.group(1),
            'span': [m.start(), m.end()],
            'raw': m.group(0),
        })
    
    # {variable} references (but not inside #object/{field} which was already captured)
    for m in re.finditer(r'(?<!/)\{([^}]+)\}', prose):
        # Skip if this is part of a #reference
        if m.start() > 0 and prose[m.start()-1] == '/':
            continue
        refs.append({
            'id': f'ref_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}',
            'type': 'variable',
            'slug': '{' + m.group(1) + '}',
            'span': [m.start(), m.end()],
            'raw': m.group(0),
        })
    
    # agent-name references (hyphenated words ending in -agent, -reviewer, -bot, etc.)
    for m in re.finditer(r'\b([a-z][a-z0-9]*(?:-[a-z][a-z0-9]*)+)\b', prose):
        name = m.group(1)
        if any(name.endswith(suffix) for suffix in ('-agent', '-reviewer', '-bot', '-checker', '-monitor', '-handler')):
            refs.append({
                'id': f'ref_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}',
                'type': 'agent',
                'slug': name,
                'span': [m.start(), m.end()],
                'raw': m.group(0),
            })
    
    return refs


def _resolve_references(refs: list, catalog: list, pg: Any) -> tuple[list, list]:
    """Resolve references against the catalog. Returns (resolved, unresolved_slugs)."""
    catalog_map = {r['slug']: r for r in catalog}
    resolved = []
    unresolved = []
    
    for ref in refs:
        slug = ref['slug']
        cat_entry = catalog_map.get(slug)
        
        if cat_entry:
            ref['resolved_to'] = cat_entry.get('resolved_id')
            ref['resolved_table'] = cat_entry.get('resolved_table')
            ref['display_name'] = cat_entry.get('display_name')
            ref['description'] = cat_entry.get('description', '')
            resolved.append(ref)
        elif ref['type'] == 'variable':
            # Variables don't need catalog resolution
            resolved.append(ref)
        elif ref['type'] == 'agent':
            # Agents are named by the user, don't need catalog resolution
            resolved.append(ref)
        else:
            # Try partial match (e.g., @gmail might match @gmail/search)
            partial = [c for c in catalog if c['slug'].startswith(slug + '/')]
            if partial:
                ref['resolved_to'] = partial[0].get('resolved_id')
                ref['display_name'] = partial[0].get('display_name')
                ref['partial_matches'] = [p['slug'] for p in partial]
                resolved.append(ref)
            else:
                unresolved.append(f'{slug} — not found in reference catalog')
                ref['resolved_to'] = None
                resolved.append(ref)
    
    return resolved, unresolved


def _generate_execution_plan(compiled: dict, references: list, pg: Any) -> dict:
    """Convert compiled prose + resolved references into a QueueSpec."""
    prose = compiled.get('prose', '')
    title = compiled.get('title', 'Untitled')
    
    # Group references by type
    integrations = [r for r in references if r['type'] == 'integration' and r.get('resolved_to')]
    agents = [r for r in references if r['type'] == 'agent']
    objects = [r for r in references if r['type'] == 'object']
    
    jobs = []
    triggers = []
    
    # Detect trigger from prose (first @integration reference that looks like an event source)
    trigger_patterns = ['receives', 'arrives', 'comes in', 'is filed', 'is created', 'fires', 'triggers']
    for ref in integrations:
        slug = ref['slug']
        # Check if the text around this reference mentions triggering
        start = max(0, ref['span'][0] - 50)
        end = min(len(prose), ref['span'][1] + 50)
        context = prose[start:end].lower()
        if any(p in context for p in trigger_patterns):
            triggers.append({
                'event_type': 'manual',  # Will be configured by user
                'source_ref': slug,
                'description': f'Triggered by {slug}',
            })
            break
    
    # Create jobs from agent references
    prev_label = None
    for i, agent_ref in enumerate(agents):
        agent_name = agent_ref['slug']
        # Find the section of prose this agent is responsible for
        start = agent_ref['span'][0]
        # Find next agent or end of prose
        end = agents[i+1]['span'][0] if i+1 < len(agents) else len(prose)
        section = prose[start:end].strip()
        
        # Determine agent route from name
        if 'review' in agent_name or 'quality' in agent_name or 'check' in agent_name:
            route = 'auto/review'
        elif 'triage' in agent_name or 'classify' in agent_name:
            route = 'auto/architecture'
        elif 'notify' in agent_name or 'send' in agent_name or 'route' in agent_name:
            route = 'auto/wiring'
        else:
            route = 'auto/build'
        
        label = agent_name.replace('-', '_')
        job = {
            'label': label,
            'agent': route,
            'prompt': f'## Operating Model Step: {agent_name}\n\n{section}\n\n## Full Context\n{prose}',
            'agent_name': agent_name,
        }
        
        # Add context from object references
        relevant_objects = [o for o in objects if o['span'][0] >= start and o['span'][0] < end]
        if relevant_objects:
            job['prompt'] += '\n\n## Data References\n' + '\n'.join(f'- {o["slug"]}' for o in relevant_objects)
        
        # Add integration references
        relevant_integrations = [r for r in integrations if r['span'][0] >= start and r['span'][0] < end]
        if relevant_integrations:
            # Check if this should be an integration job instead
            for int_ref in relevant_integrations:
                if int_ref.get('resolved_to'):
                    parts = int_ref['slug'].lstrip('@').split('/')
                    if len(parts) == 2:
                        job['integration_id'] = parts[0]
                        job['integration_action'] = parts[1]
                        job['integration_args'] = {}
                        job['agent'] = f'integration/{parts[0]}'
        
        if prev_label:
            job['depends_on'] = [prev_label]
        
        jobs.append(job)
        prev_label = label
    
    # If no agents were found, create a single job from the whole prose
    if not jobs:
        jobs.append({
            'label': 'execute',
            'agent': 'auto/build',
            'prompt': f'## Operating Model\n\n{prose}',
        })
    
    return {
        'name': title,
        'queue_id': f'om_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:8]}',
        'phase': 'build',
        'outcome_goal': title,
        'jobs': jobs,
        'triggers': triggers,
    }
```

## IMPORTANT
- Read `adapters/llm_client.py` first to understand the LLMRequest interface
- The LLM call should be optional — if no API key, fall back to basic compilation
- Use the existing `call_llm` function, not raw HTTP calls
- Reference extraction uses regex, not the LLM — it's deterministic and fast
- The execution plan generates QueueSpec-compatible jobs
- Handle all edge cases: no agents found, no integrations, empty prose

jobs.label: compile_commit_api
jobs.prompt:
## Objective
Add POST /api/compile and POST /api/commit API endpoints.

## Working Directory
`Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/api/handlers/workflow_query.py`

## Context
The compiler is at `runtime/compiler.py` with function `compile_prose(prose, pg) -> dict`.

The submit_dispatch_inline function is at `runtime/workflow/unified.py` — it takes `(conn, spec_dict, run_id, parent_run_id, trigger_depth)` and inserts into Postgres.

Existing handler pattern: `def handler(request, path) -> None:`, uses `request.subsystems.get_pg_conn()`, `request._send_json(status, data)`, reads JSON body from request.

## Add to workflow_query.py:

### POST /api/compile
Compile natural language prose into a structured operating model.

Handler:
```python
def _handle_compile_post(request, path):
    try:
        body = json.loads(request.body) if hasattr(request, 'body') and request.body else {}
        prose = body.get('prose', '')
        if not prose.strip():
            request._send_json(400, {'error': 'prose is required'})
            return
        
        pg = request.subsystems.get_pg_conn()
        from runtime.materializer import compile_prose
        result = compile_prose(prose, pg)
        request._send_json(200, result)
    except Exception as exc:
        request._send_json(500, {'error': str(exc)})
```

### POST /api/commit
Commit a compiled operating model — save to workflows table and optionally create triggers.

Handler:
```python
def _handle_commit_post(request, path):
    try:
        body = json.loads(request.body) if hasattr(request, 'body') and request.body else {}
        prose = body.get('prose', '')
        compiled_prose = body.get('compiled_prose', prose)
        references = body.get('references', [])
        execution_plan = body.get('execution_plan', {})
        title = body.get('title', prose[:60])
        workflow_id = body.get('workflow_id')
        authority = body.get('authority', '')
        sla = body.get('sla', {})
        
        pg = request.subsystems.get_pg_conn()
        
        # Save to workflows table
        if not workflow_id:
            workflow_id = 'wf_' + uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]
        
        definition = {
            'type': 'operating_model',
            'prose': compiled_prose,
            'references': references,
            'authority': authority,
            'sla': sla,
        }
        
        pg.execute(
            """INSERT INTO workflows (id, name, description, definition, compiled_spec, version)
               VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4::jsonb, $5::jsonb, 1)
               ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET
                 name = EXCLUDED.name,
                 definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
                 compiled_spec = EXCLUDED.compiled_spec,
                 version = workflows.version + 1,
                 updated_at = now()""",
            workflow_id, title, compiled_prose[:200],
            json.dumps(definition), json.dumps(execution_plan),
        )
        
        # Create triggers if specified
        triggers = execution_plan.get('triggers', [])
        for trig in triggers:
            trig_id = 'trg_' + uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]
            pg.execute(
                """INSERT INTO workflow_triggers (id, workflow_id, event_type, filter, enabled)
                   VALUES ($1, $2, $3, $4::jsonb, true)
                   ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING""",
                trig_id, workflow_id, trig.get('event_type', 'manual'),
                json.dumps(trig.get('filter', {})),
            )
        
        request._send_json(200, {
            'workflow_id': workflow_id,
            'status': 'committed',
            'title': title,
            'jobs': len(execution_plan.get('jobs', [])),
            'triggers': len(triggers),
        })
    except Exception as exc:
        request._send_json(500, {'error': str(exc)})
```

### GET /api/references
List all available references from the catalog (for autocomplete).

```python
def _handle_references_get(request, path):
    try:
        pg = request.subsystems.get_pg_conn()
        ref_type = None  # could filter by query param
        rows = pg.execute('SELECT slug, ref_type, display_name, description FROM reference_catalog ORDER BY ref_type, slug')
        refs = [dict(r) for r in (rows or [])]
        request._send_json(200, {'references': refs, 'count': len(refs)})
    except Exception as exc:
        request._send_json(500, {'error': str(exc)})
```

Add these to the route lists:
- `_handle_compile_post` → QUERY_POST_ROUTES with `_exact('/api/compile')`
- `_handle_commit_post` → QUERY_POST_ROUTES with `_exact('/api/commit')`
- `_handle_references_get` → QUERY_GET_ROUTES with `_exact('/api/references')`

## IMPORTANT
- Read workflow_query.py first to understand how other handlers read the request body
- Check how POST handlers parse JSON — it might be `request.body`, `request._read_body()`, or similar
- Import uuid and json at the top if not already imported
- Add the routes to the correct lists (GET vs POST)

jobs.label: operating_model_editor
wire_into_app
jobs.prompt:
## Objective Wire the OperatingModelEditor into the App.tsx mode system and update the Dashboard. ## Working Directory `Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/` ## Context App.tsx has a mode system: 'dashboard' | 'build' | 'run-detail'. It needs a new mode 'edit-model' for the operating model editor. Dashboard.tsx has a "+ New Workflow" button and a "Describe It" button that should open the operating model editor. The OperatingModelEditor component is at `dashboard/OperatingModelEditor.tsx`. ## Task 1: Update App.tsx Read App.tsx first. Add 'edit-model' to the AppMode type: ```typescript export type AppMode = 'dashboard' | 'build' | 'run-detail' | 'edit-model'; ``` Add lazy import for OperatingModelEditor: ```typescript const OperatingModelEditor = React.lazy(() => import('./dashboard/OperatingModelEditor').then(m => ({ default: m.OperatingModelEditor })).catch(() => ({ default: () =>
Editor loading...
})) ); ``` Add the mode case in the render: ```tsx {state.mode === 'edit-model' && ( navigate('dashboard')} onCommitted={(id) => navigate('dashboard')} /> )} ``` ## Task 2: Update Dashboard.tsx Read Dashboard.tsx. The "Describe It" button and "+ New Workflow" should open the operating model editor. Add a new prop to DashboardProps: ```typescript onDescribe: () => void; // opens operating model editor ``` Change the "Describe It" button: ```tsx ``` Change the sidebar "+ New Workflow" to also open the editor (or keep it opening the builder — user can choose).
